decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since material tuning has stalled, I will implement a coordinated geometry and material change: increase the heat spreader width to cover more area above the chip and simultaneously increase its conductivity to maximize lateral spreading efficiency.

changes.reason: Widening the spreader increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat flow, reducing thermal resistance from the chip to the cooled boundaries.
Increasing conductivity by 25% (within 2x limit) enhances the spreader's ability to distribute heat laterally, working synergistically with the increased width.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Improved heat extraction efficiency towards left/right Dirichlet boundaries
risk_notes: Spreader width is approaching the design domain limit (1.2m); future geometric expansions may be constrained.
Diminishing returns on conductivity increases if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions.
